name: python3-rq
title: python3-rq
description:
Simple job queues for Python 3
RQ (Redis Queue) is a simple Python library for queueing jobs and
processing them in the background with workers. It is backed by
Redis and it is designed to have a low barrier to entry.
It can be integrated in your web stack easily.

commands.name: rqworker
commands.description: Command line monitor to start an RQ worker.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use rq because Simple job queues for Python 3.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use rq as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `rq [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS] ...` as documented upstream for rq.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair rq with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `rq --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install python-rq. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `rq [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS] ...`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Simple job queues for Python 3.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: Simple job queues for Python 3.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install python-rq
Install with: sudo apt install python-rq
